def fetch_records(last_created_at, last_id):
    # Correct: always include a unique, sequential ID column as a tiebreaker in the ORDER BY clause
    query = """
        SELECT * FROM records 
        WHERE (created_at, id) > (?, ?) 
        ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC 
        LIMIT 10
    """
    return db.execute(query, (last_created_at, last_id))
